display.purpose: Mastheads, profile names, trend headlines, pull-quotes. Variable axes opsz 144 + WONK 1 at hero sizes.

body.purpose: Prose, table cells, form labels, UI copy.

mono.purpose: Every number, section slugs, timestamps, rank numbers. Tabular numerals via .tnum.

motion.name: reduced-motion floor
motion.purpose: Every animation (SilkCanvas, Tape, Reveal, CountUp, Sparkline, silk-drift) has a static floor; content is always visible without JS.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Amber fill with canvas-deep (near-black) text — white text on amber fails AA. Hover brightens to Amber Bright.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #e3b04b; color: #0a0506; font-weight: 600; padding: 0.625rem 1.25rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; border: none; transition: background-color 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #f0c96a; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e3b04b; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Panel
components.description: The single surface primitive: surface fill, faint hairline, inset top highlight instead of a drop shadow. Used sparingly — most sections sit directly on canvas under a slug header.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: #1b1214; border: 1px solid #271b1d; border-radius: 0.625rem; box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(244,237,226,0.04); padding: 1.5rem; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Dark translucent fill, hairline border that turns amber on focus, no ring. Autofill overridden to stay dark.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; background: rgba(27,18,20,0.8); border: 1px solid #362527; border-radius: 0.375rem; padding: 0.625rem 0.75rem; color: #f4ede2; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: #e3b04b; }

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Muted at rest; active link is ivory with an amber underline.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { color: #b5a69b; font-size: 0.875rem; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-nav-link:hover, .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { color: #f4ede2; } .ds-nav-link[aria-current="page"] { color: #f4ede2; box-shadow: 0 2px 0 #e3b04b; }

components.name: Section Slug
components.description: Mono uppercase tracked label used AS the section heading (newsroom-wire cadence) — never as an eyebrow above a second heading.
components.css: .ds-slug { font-family: 'Spline Sans Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #e3b04b; }

narrative.overview: A cinematic, nocturnal, editorial instrument — the night watch for a roster of stars. The scraper runs while Madrid sleeps, and the interface embraces that: a deep garnet-black canvas, warm ivory type, and one gilded signal color. It reads like a couture house's internal terminal — a fashion-magazine masthead married to a trading floor's tape. Triage still comes first: 'who needs attention' is answerable at a glance. The presentation is a deliberate showcase of craft (WebGL silk shader, view-transition morphs, draw-on sparklines, odometer count-ups), but motion always enhances already-visible content and always respects prefers-reduced-motion.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black garnet canvas (#120b0d); depth via surface tone steps and hairlines, not shadows.
One accent — Gilded Amber — meaning 'signal', never decoration in bulk.
Three type voices with three jobs: Fraunces speaks, Archivo explains, Spline Sans Mono measures.
Semantic mint/vermillion reserved strictly for trend direction and alarm.
A fixed film-grain overlay (.grain) gives every page its nocturnal texture.

rules.name: The Signal Rule
rules.body: Amber is scarce: slugs, links, the active nav underline, one button. If amber exceeds a tenth of a screen, something is decorated, not designed.

rules.name: The Noise Rule
rules.body: Deltas under max(5, 0.01% of audience) render neutral — alarm colors are reserved for meaningful movement.

rules.name: The Three Voices Rule
rules.body: Serif speaks (titles, quotes), grotesque explains (prose, UI), mono measures (numbers, labels). Never swap jobs.

dos: Do keep every number in mono with .tnum.
Do gate alarm colors behind the Noise Rule.
Do give every animation a reduced-motion floor and keep content visible pre-JS.
Do use full exact numerals when a chart's range is too tight for compact ticks.
donts: Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism-as-decoration, or drop shadows — the panel's inset top highlight is the only elevation cue.
Don't put white text on amber (2.5:1) — amber fills take canvas-deep text.
Don't add a second accent hue; garnet is a material, not a message.
Don't use the mono slug as an eyebrow above another heading — it IS the heading.
Don't warm the canvas toward brown/cream; it stays garnet-black.
